sections.paragraph:
(with Tsere impure) m.-

(1) a wolf, because it frightens the flock (unless the verb be a denominative). [“So called from its tawny and yellow colour.” Thes] Arab. ذِيءْبُ, Syr. ܕܺܐܒܐܳ. Gen 49:27 Isa 11:6 65:25 Jer 5:6, זְאֵבֵי עֶרֶב “evening wolves,” those which go forth to prowl at evening. Hab 1:8 Zep 3:3, comp. λύκοι νυκτερινοί, Oppian. Cyneget. iii. 206, νυκτιπόροι ibid. i. 440.

(2) [Zeeb], pr.n. of a Midianite prince, Jud. 7:25 8:3 Psa 83:12.
